Import TensorFlow, NumPy, and other required libraries from syntaxnet/reader_ops_test.py
Load MNIST dataset using TensorFlow's datasets API and preprocess it for input into a spatial transform network model
Initialize a GreedyParser instance with num_actions=62 (assuming this is a common action space for MNIST), num_features=[784], num_feature_ids=[256], embedding_sizes=[128], hidden_layer_sizes=[32]
Call the AddTraining method of the GreedyParser instance to prepare it for training, specifying corpus_name='MNIST_corpus'
Initialize all variables using the inits.values() method of the GreedyParser instance
Create a placeholder for input data with shape (batch_size, 784)
Call the train_op operation within the training dictionary of the GreedyParser instance to perform a single training step
Fetch the cost from the training dictionary of the GreedyParser instance after each training step
Assert that the fetched cost contains NaN values during at least one training epoch
Monitor GPU memory usage using TensorFlow's device placement logging during the training loop
Verify that the peak GPU memory consumption exceeds 10GB
